questions.text: What is the goal of this study?
choices: Diagnostic: estimating current disease status or distinguishing between patients with and without a disease
Screening: identifying asymptomatic or general-population individuals who are likely to have or develop a disease
Prognostic: predicting future clinical outcomes for patients with a particular disease
Etiologic: identifying factors associated with or causing disease or health outcomes
Treatment effect: evaluating the effect of an intervention or comparing interventions
Descriptive: describing disease prevalence, incidence, characteristics, or trends without primarily making predictions
Other: the goal of the study does not fit any of the other categories

questions.text: What is the cohort size of the study?
In what way is the cohort described in this study, for example regarding age, gender, disease stage, demographic variables, socioeconomic status, and so on?
What information about the dataset is provided in this study, for example regarding the number of features, provenance, data quality, missing values, and so on?
What type of data or data modalities are used in this study?
choices: Tabular data: structured data represented as rows and columns
Time-series data: data points that have temporal relationships between them
Image data: data consisting of images
Text data: data consisting of (free) text
Audio data: data consisting of audio recordings
Video data: data consisting of video recordings
Multimodal data: data consisting of multiple modalities (e.g., tabular, text, image, audio)
Other: the data or data modalities do not fit any of the other categories
